task.title: Add optional project/user/private context templates with privacy warnings.

context.problem_statement: Some target repos need a place to record project preferences, user preferences, and private local context for agents, but repo-contract-kit must make the safe path explicit so secrets and personal context are not accidentally committed or copied into hosted prompts.
background: AGW-035 is a repo-contract-kit backlog item with delivery shape: Templates and gitignore guidance.

repo-contract-kit already owns installer profiles, target-repo managed templates, runtime adapter routing, local workflow docs, instruction linting, and target gitignore scaffolds for run/task sidecar state.
agent-workflow-kit already has local/private review policy guidance, but this task belongs in repo-contract-kit because it installs target-repo files and update-safe managed profile content.
Existing installer profiles are explicit and composable; privacy-sensitive context should not be part of default, minimal, or agentic preset installation without a separate decision.
The current local-agentic profile installs workflow mechanics but no dedicated private context templates or ignore rules.
non_goals: Do not add this profile to the default, minimal, learning, test-first, or agentic presets.
Do not install real user context, secrets, credentials, account identifiers, private URLs, customer data, personal messages, or machine-specific paths.
Do not create a memory system, Keryx/Obsidian integration, browser profile integration, hosted sync, secret scanner, or external planning adapter.
Do not change agent-workflow-kit canonical prompts, schemas, generated adapters, or runtime adapter source files.
Do not change installed review-runner, task-runner, context-bundle, preflight, self-heal, or lifecycle behavior.
Do not make linting or docs checks read ignored private context content by default.

goal_alignment.repo_goal: Keep repo-contract-kit as the install-layer guardrail provider for target repos, with explicit opt-in profiles, update-safe managed files, local-first workflow docs, and privacy-preserving defaults.

area_contracts.purpose: Composable optional install profiles and profile-owned target-repo templates.
Resolves explicit profiles, copies managed template files, and records install receipt/manifest metadata.
Installed local policy for tools, network, secrets, accounts, and trust profile boundaries.
Installed operator workflow docs where optional context templates and privacy warnings should be discoverable.
Installer regression tests for explicit profiles, preset membership, managed manifests, and installed files.

stop_conditions: Stop if the implementation would install private context by default or include it in existing presets.
Stop if generated target files encourage writing secrets, tokens, cookies, customer data, personal messages, private URLs, or credentials.
Stop if private local context files are tracked by default instead of ignored or example-only.
Stop if the worker needs to change agent-workflow-kit prompt source or runtime adapters.
Stop if docs or tests would require reading ignored private context by default.
Stop if profile/update behavior cannot preserve customized managed files or target-owned files.

protected_files: /Volumes/Myrtle/Code/04_Code/Hermes/doc-contract-kit/scripts/agent_start.py unless tests prove startup docs must name the new optional profile
/Volumes/Myrtle/Code/04_Code/Hermes/doc-contract-kit/templates/profiles/local-agentic/manifest.json unless the worker only links to opt-in docs without installing private files by default
/Volumes/Myrtle/Code/04_Code/Hermes/doc-contract-kit/scripts/install.py preset membership except tests proving private-context is absent from presets
target-owned VERSION and CHANGELOG.md in installed target repos
real private context files without .example suffix
unrelated backlog rows and unrelated dirty work
expected_outputs: An explicit optional profile, likely private-context, installable with python3 scripts/install.py <target> --profile private-context or composed through --profiles.
Managed example templates for project, user, and private context that contain privacy warnings and placeholders only, not real local context.
A managed .gitignore in the private-context directory that tracks README/example files but ignores real local context files by default.
Template guidance that separates shareable project context from personal/user/private context and says what must never be recorded.

Docs explaining install syntax, safe use, what remains ignored, how to review before sharing, and how this differs from AGENTS.md, REVIEW.md, runtime adapters, Keryx/Obsidian memory, and secrets management.

Installer tests proving the profile installs explicitly, can compose with local-agentic/review-prompts, is absent from all presets, records managed manifest entries, and ignores real private context files in a temp git repo.
Version and changelog updates in repo-contract-kit for the new profile/template behavior.
Source backlog rows, repo-contract-kit split backlog, feature matrix, and summary close AGW-035 only after kit validation passes.

acceptance_criteria.description: The optional private-context profile installs explicitly and is absent from default/minimal/learning/test-first/agentic presets.
acceptance_criteria.verification: Focused install tests inspect profile selection and preset membership.

acceptance_criteria.description: Installed profile files provide project, user, and private context examples with clear privacy warnings and no real secret, credential, account, private URL, customer data, or personal-message placeholders that look live.
acceptance_criteria.verification: Review templates and run instruction lint/docs checks.

acceptance_criteria.description: The installed context directory tracks only README/example guidance and ignores real local context files by default.
acceptance_criteria.verification: Install into a temporary git repo, create real context files, and verify git status ignores them while example files remain visible/managed.

acceptance_criteria.description: Docs explain how to use the templates safely, how to review/redact before sharing, and why the profile is opt-in rather than part of normal agentic installs.
acceptance_criteria.verification: Inspect README, rollout/operator docs, and installed workflow docs.

acceptance_criteria.description: Update behavior remains safe: managed template changes are tracked in the manifest and customized managed files are not overwritten silently.
acceptance_criteria.verification: Focused update/install tests or existing update tests pass.

acceptance_criteria.description: The change does not add memory integrations, prompt-source changes, runtime adapter changes, or runner behavior.
acceptance_criteria.verification: Run git diff --name-only in both repos and inspect against the allowed/protected scope.

acceptance_criteria.description: repo-contract-kit version/changelog records the new opt-in profile, and source backlog closeout records AGW-035 only after kit validation and push.
acceptance_criteria.verification: Run kit make version-check and source backlog checks.

docs_impact.notes: This changes installed profile/template behavior in repo-contract-kit. Source docs updates are limited to research/backlog closeout.

known_risks: Templates can normalize storing sensitive data in repos if warnings and ignore rules are weak.
Adding private context to default presets would unexpectedly expose the feature in target repos.
A .gitignore mistake could leave real user/private context files staged or committed.
Instruction lint or token-budget checks could accidentally read local private files if they are not excluded or example-only.
Runtime-specific memory semantics can be confused with repo-contract-kit managed instructions unless docs keep this as optional local context.
stop_conditions: Any profile file contains realistic secrets, tokens, account identifiers, private URLs, or personal data.
Real private context files are tracked by default in the install smoke.
The implementation requires prompt-source, runner, or runtime-adapter changes.
The profile must be added to the agentic preset to work.
Kit validation fails or source/kit remotes diverge.

approval.notes: Active user goal is to add fully planned backlog packets and implement remaining workflow-stack items serially with one worker per backlog item.

token_budget.guidance: Keep the source packet compact and hand the worker to repo-contract-kit. The implementation should avoid stuffing private context guidance into AGENTS.md.

handoff.recommended_prompt: workflows/prompts/task-packet.md, then workflows/prompts/fix-implementer.md for the repo-contract-kit profile/template slice
dependencies: repo-contract-kit local checkout at /Volumes/Myrtle/Code/04_Code/Hermes/doc-contract-kit must remain clean before launch.
Source backlog closeout happens after repo-contract-kit implementation validation and push.
Generated private context examples must remain example-only and real private files must be ignored.
handoff.next_packet_hint: After AGW-035 closes, continue with the next open backlog item selected by make agent-next.
